title: Alternative fig-retention rules
text: illustrate extreme host-sanction setting. set host-sanction threshold 10% fig’s total flowers. number ovules occupied eggs ovule-occupying guilds (pollinators + gallers) exceeds 10% fig’s flower count, fig drops. fig drops, fig wasps emerge. example demonstration ; practice, threshold calibrated empirical biology. recent nutrient sink strength hypothesis fig abortion also adopted simulation (Segar et al. 2025), can activated (use_sink_strength = TRUE). quantified sink strength galler species, including pollinator non-pollinator galler, galler species directly utilize flowers parasitoid wasps use flowers. simplify model, assume sink strength pollinating non-pollinating gallers, relationship number galler wasps sink strength linear (sink_linear_coef, default = 1; relationship unlikely strictly linear reality can adjusted needed). comparison, sink strength contributions gallers seeds weighted two parameters calculate total sink strength within fig. default weights galler sink strength (sink_w_gall) seed sink strength (sink_w_seed) set 1 1.5, respectively, following observation reported previously (Wang et al. 2014, Wang et al. 2020b). , fig-level sink strength exceeds allowable range (reflecting nutrient energy allocation fig), sink_min_prop (default = 0.2) sink_max_prop (default = 0.95), fig marked aborted. Notes: () Parasitoid eggs counted toward ovule-occupation total occupy ovules directly. (ii) function still uses legacy argument name host_sanction, use ; otherwise use corrected host_sanction_threshold. title: Alternative fig-retention rules
code: # First, ensure the sanction mechanism *enable_sanctions <- TRUE * # Run the simulation with this specific sanction rule # Key change: set a strict host-sanction threshold at 10% of total flowers # If ovule-occupying eggs (pollinators + gallers) > 10% of flowers -> fig drops -> zero emergence. # We mark figs as dropped but keep eggs/emergence for inspection (drop_cancels_emergence = FALSE). # For analysis, we create a masked copy where dropped figs' emergence counts are zeroed.  # LHS over thousands of samples × 1000‑fig simulations is expensive. In the vignette we either (A) load a precomputed result, or (B) run a tiny demo search. For full runs, set `RUN_HEAVY <- TRUE` and increase `n_samples`, `n_draws`, `sample_n`, and `num_figs`.  RUN_HEAVY <- FALSE  sim_sanction_10 <- simulate_figwasp_community(   num_figs = if (RUN_HEAVY) 1000 else 100,   fecundity_mean       = parameter_list_default$fecundity_mean,   fecundity_dispersion = parameter_list_default$fecundity_dispersion,   entry_mu             = parameter_list_default$entry_mu,   entry_size           = parameter_list_default$entry_size,   entry_priority       = parameter_list_default$entry_priority,   species_roles        = parameter_list_default$species_roles,   max_entry_table      = parameter_list_default$max_entry_table,   enable_drop          = TRUE,   drop_cancels_emergence = FALSE, # keep it as FALSE: it means "drop => zero emergence"   entry_distribution   = "lognormal",   interaction_matrix   = parameter_list_default$interaction_matrix,   interaction_weight   = 0,   host_sanction = 0.1, # the key change, default = 0.8   egg_success_prob     = parameter_list_default$egg_success_prob,   egg_success_prob_by_phase = parameter_list_default$egg_success_prob_by_phase,   layer_preference     = parameter_list_default$layer_preference,   use_layering         = TRUE,  # let's turn it on   # new feature: sink metrics   use_sink_strength = FALSE, # disable sink strength   sink_w_gall          = 1.0,   sink_w_seed          = 1.5,   sink_linear_coef     = 1.0,   sink_min_prop        = 0.20,   sink_max_prop        = 0.95,   seed                 = 43 )  sim_sanction_10 <- sim_sanction_10$summary  # Quick check: how many figs dropped, and confirm dropped figs have zero emergence summarize_simulated_metrics(sim_sanction_10, species_list, version_label = "sanction 10%")$drop_summary #>  #>  0  1  #> 12 88

title: figsimR: An R Package for Simulating Fig–wasp Community Dynamics
text: Ecologists long recognized natural communities exhibit large variability. figsimR operationalizes mechanistic null workflow: calibrate baseline configuration modeling (BCM) reproduce empirical means, quantify Variation Gap—residual dispersion intrinsic model capture. Finally, use mechanism knockout experiments dissect internal processes maintain theoretical structure. vignette shows full workflow fig–wasp case study, small defaults speed. full reproduction (long runs), see package website article “Reproducing case study” (run vignette RUN_HEAVY <- TRUE).

what: 3.3 Explore parameter space (short demo vs. full)
title: figsimR: An R Package for Simulating Fig–wasp Community Dynamics
text: Important: LHS thousands samples × 1000‑fig simulations expensive. vignette either () load precomputed result, (B) run tiny demo search. full runs, set RUN_HEAVY <- TRUE increase n_samples, n_draws, sample_n, num_figs.
code: # Optional: progress bars (kept OFF in vignette) # progressr::handlers(global = TRUE); progressr::handlers("txtprogressbar")  # load a precomputed optimization (ship it in inst/extdata/) lhs_file <- system.file("extdata", "lhs_optimize_result.rds", package = "figsimR")  if (file.exists(lhs_file)) {   lhs_opt <- readRDS(lhs_file) } else if (RUN_HEAVY) {   lhs_opt <- explore_parameter_space_lhs(     new_param_ranges        = param_ranges,     observed_summary        = obs_metrics[, 1:10], # numeric summary columns     num_figs                = 1000,     n_draws                 = 10,     sample_n                = 50,     wasp_cols               = species_list,     parameter_list_template = parameter_list_default,     n_samples               = 5000,     top_k                   = 5,     n_cores                 = max(1, parallel::detectCores() - 1)   ) } else {      lhs_opt <- explore_parameter_space_lhs(     new_param_ranges        = param_ranges,     observed_summary        = obs_metrics[, 1:10],     num_figs                = 200,     n_draws                 = 10,     sample_n                = 50,     wasp_cols               = species_list,     parameter_list_template = parameter_list_default,     n_samples               = 50,         top_k                   = 1,     n_cores                 = 1   ) }  best_row <- lhs_opt[1, , drop = FALSE] best_params <- rebuild_parameter_list_from_row(best_row, parameter_list_default)

code: # Example: remove spatial layering sim_no_space <- simulate_figwasp_community(   num_figs = if (RUN_HEAVY) 1000 else 100,   fecundity_mean       = best_params$fecundity_mean,   fecundity_dispersion = best_params$fecundity_dispersion,   entry_mu             = best_params$entry_mu,   entry_size           = best_params$entry_size,   entry_priority       = best_params$entry_priority,   species_roles        = best_params$species_roles,   max_entry_table      = best_params$max_entry_table,   enable_drop          = TRUE,   drop_cancels_emergence = FALSE,   entry_distribution   = "lognormal",   interaction_matrix   = best_params$interaction_matrix,   interaction_weight   = 0,   egg_success_prob     = best_params$egg_success_prob,   egg_success_prob_by_phase = best_params$egg_success_prob_by_phase,   layer_preference     = best_params$layer_preference,   use_layering         = FALSE,  # KO here   seed                 = 43 )$summary  # Summarize metrics for BCM vs. KO bcm_metrics  <- resample_metrics_from_simulation(sim_df,       n_reps = 50, sample_size = 100, seed = 1) ko_metrics   <- resample_metrics_from_simulation(sim_no_space, n_reps = 50, sample_size = 100, seed = 1)  # Loss vs empirical means (smaller = better to observed) obs_means_row <- as.data.frame(t(colMeans(obs_metrics[ , setdiff(names(obs_metrics), "source") ], na.rm = TRUE))) bcm_loss      <- calculate_metric_loss(bcm_metrics, obs_means_row) ko_loss       <- calculate_metric_loss(ko_metrics,  obs_means_row)  list(BCM_total_loss = bcm_loss$total_loss,      KO_no_space_total_loss = ko_loss$total_loss) #> $BCM_total_loss #> [1] 533.9441 #>  #> $KO_no_space_total_loss #> [1] 462.0761

title: figsimR: An R Package for Simulating Fig–wasp Community Dynamics
text: bigger loss jump module removed, important module reproducing community structure within intrinsic envelope. use ‘intrinsic envelope’ purely modeling term; imply processes stochastic nature—yet modeled .
code: # First, ensure the sanction mechanism *enable_sanctions <- TRUE * # Run the simulation with this specific sanction rule # Key change: set a strict host-sanction threshold at 10% of total flowers # If ovule-occupying eggs (pollinators + gallers) > 10% of flowers -> fig drops -> zero emergence. # We mark figs as dropped but keep eggs/emergence for inspection (drop_cancels_emergence = FALSE). # For analysis, we create a masked copy where dropped figs' emergence counts are zeroed.  sim_sanction_10 <- simulate_figwasp_community(   num_figs = if (RUN_HEAVY) 1000 else 100,   fecundity_mean       = best_params$fecundity_mean,   fecundity_dispersion = best_params$fecundity_dispersion,   entry_mu             = best_params$entry_mu,   entry_size           = best_params$entry_size,   entry_priority       = best_params$entry_priority,   species_roles        = best_params$species_roles,   max_entry_table      = best_params$max_entry_table,   enable_drop          = TRUE,   drop_cancels_emergence = FALSE, # keep it as FALSE: it means "drop => zero emergence"   entry_distribution   = "lognormal",   interaction_matrix   = best_params$interaction_matrix,   interaction_weight   = 0,   host_sanction = 0.1, # the key change, default = 0.8   egg_success_prob     = best_params$egg_success_prob,   egg_success_prob_by_phase = best_params$egg_success_prob_by_phase,   layer_preference     = best_params$layer_preference,   use_layering         = TRUE,  # let's turn it on   # new feature: sink metrics   use_sink_strength = FALSE,   seed                 = 43 )  sim_sanction_10 <- sim_sanction_10$summary  # Quick check: how many figs dropped, and confirm dropped figs have zero emergence summarize_simulated_metrics(sim_sanction_10, species_list, version_label = "sanction 10%")$drop_summary #>  #>  0  1  #> 28 72

text: Let define parameter check function first
code: validate_figsim_parameter_list <- function(parameter_list) {    required_components <- c(     "entry_mu",     "entry_size",     "fecundity_mean",     "fecundity_dispersion",     "egg_success_prob",     "egg_success_prob_by_phase",     "layer_preference",     "max_entry_table",     "parasitism_prob",     "entry_priority",     "interaction_matrix",     "interaction_weight",     "species_roles"   )    missing_components <- setdiff(required_components, names(parameter_list))   if (length(missing_components) > 0) {     stop(       "Missing required components: ",       paste(missing_components, collapse = ", ")     )   }    species_roles <- parameter_list$species_roles   species_names <- names(species_roles$guild)    if (length(species_names) == 0) {     stop("species_roles$guild must be a named vector.")   }    # Check species-indexed numeric vectors   species_vectors <- c(     "entry_mu",     "entry_size",     "fecundity_mean",     "fecundity_dispersion",     "egg_success_prob",     "max_entry_table"   )    for (v in species_vectors) {     missing_species <- setdiff(species_names, names(parameter_list[[v]]))     extra_species <- setdiff(names(parameter_list[[v]]), species_names)      if (length(missing_species) > 0) {       stop(v, " is missing species: ", paste(missing_species, collapse = ", "))     }      if (length(extra_species) > 0) {       warning(v, " contains extra species: ", paste(extra_species, collapse = ", "))     }   }    # Check hosts and parasitoid lists   if (!all(species_names %in% names(species_roles$hosts))) {     stop("species_roles$hosts must contain all species.")   }    if (!all(species_names %in% names(species_roles$parasitoid))) {     stop("species_roles$parasitoid must contain all species.")   }    all_hosts <- unique(unlist(species_roles$hosts))   all_parasitoids <- unique(unlist(species_roles$parasitoid))    invalid_hosts <- setdiff(all_hosts, species_names)   invalid_parasitoids <- setdiff(all_parasitoids, species_names)    if (length(invalid_hosts) > 0) {     stop("Unknown host species: ", paste(invalid_hosts, collapse = ", "))   }    if (length(invalid_parasitoids) > 0) {     stop("Unknown parasitoid species: ", paste(invalid_parasitoids, collapse = ", "))   }    # Check entry priority   entry_species <- unique(unlist(parameter_list$entry_priority))   missing_from_priority <- setdiff(species_names, entry_species)   invalid_priority_species <- setdiff(entry_species, species_names)    if (length(missing_from_priority) > 0) {     warning(       "These species are not included in entry_priority: ",       paste(missing_from_priority, collapse = ", ")     )   }    if (length(invalid_priority_species) > 0) {     stop(       "entry_priority contains unknown species: ",       paste(invalid_priority_species, collapse = ", ")     )   }    # Check interaction matrix   interaction_matrix <- parameter_list$interaction_matrix    if (!is.matrix(interaction_matrix)) {     stop("interaction_matrix must be a matrix.")   }    if (!all(species_names %in% rownames(interaction_matrix)) ||       !all(species_names %in% colnames(interaction_matrix))) {     stop("interaction_matrix rownames and colnames must include all species.")   }    # Check layer preference   if (!all(species_names %in% names(parameter_list$layer_preference))) {     stop("layer_preference must contain all species.")   }    # Check egg_success_prob_by_phase   if (!all(species_names %in% names(parameter_list$egg_success_prob_by_phase))) {     stop("egg_success_prob_by_phase must contain all species.")   }    message("Parameter list passed all checks.")   invisible(TRUE) }

title: Applying figsimR to other Ficus systems
text: Use package Ficus-fig wasp systems, prerequisite need prepare input data following package guidelines, especially species roles guilds, basic biological information, expected oviposition egg-laying rates, interactions among species, host-parasitoid relationships. fact, many fig wasp communities, information scattered across different publications remains unclear, may limit practical application. However, limitation available biological information rather restriction package.

title: Parameter guide
text: full parameter, please see help document: ?simulate_figwasp_community()

title: Parameter guide
text: Exponent power-law relationship fig diameter flower number. Empirically estimated around 1.2-1.4 many figs.

title: Parameter guide
text: Logical. FALSE, dropped figs produce zero emergence seeds. TRUE, output remains.

title: Parameter guide
text: Global probability single oviposition attempt results successful egg. Applies use_egg_success_by_phase = FALSE.

title: Parameter guide
text: Overrides global success probabilities entry phase. species-level list phase-specific reproductive-success probabilities. species developmental phase, supplied value used probability arriving oviposition-attempting female contributes eggs phase. phase-specific value supplied, model uses baseline egg_success_prob. Realized egg numbers subsequently determined fecundity, host availability, ovary-layer accessibility, resource constraints. Format: species_name = list(phase1 = 0.5, phase2 = 0.7,…).

title: Parameter guide
text: Enables fig abortion due host sanctions. flower use proportion exceeds host_sanction, fig may abort. output “drop_prob” column: Ranges 0 (never drop) 1 (always drop); “is_dropped” column: drop (0) drop (1).

title: Parameter guide
text: Either “nb” (negative binomial) “lognormal”. Defines per-species entry numbers simulated: “nb”: entries drawn NB( mu = entry_mu, size = entry_size ). “lognormal”: log(entry_mu) - 0.5 used meanlog; sdlog = 1 / sqrt(entry_size); result used Poisson rate.

title: Parameter guide
text: Mean number individuals species attempting “enter” (arrival oviposition-attempt events) fig. Acts expected value entry distribution.

title: Parameter guide
text: Defines temporal entry order species. element phase (e.g., phase1), vector species names. Earlier phases enter first may influence resource host availability later phases.

title: Parameter guide
text: Controls variation entry distribution (dispersion arrival oviposition-attempt distribution): entry_distribution = “nb”: used NB size parameter. “lognormal”: used derive sdlog = 1 / sqrt(entry_size).

title: Parameter guide
text: Dispersion parameter fecundity (size NB distribution). Controls variability egg output across individuals.

title: Parameter guide
text: Mean number eggs laid per female individual, per species. Used mu negative binomial distribution.

title: Parameter guide
text: aximum fig diameter used cap values (optional).

title: Parameter guide
text: Mean fig diameter (cm). Used generate per-fig flower number via formula: flower_count = k, diameter^alpha, Gamma(shape = 20, scale = 0.1). Larger diameters yield ovules.

title: Parameter guide
text: Truncate simulated diameters (thus flower counts) biologically reasonable range.

title: Parameter guide
text: Standard deviation fig diameter. fig’s diameter drawn normal distribution mean SD.

title: Parameter guide
text: Threshold parameter used optional host-sanction/fig-abortion module. Threshold flower use (e.g., 0.8). exceeded, fig may abort (drop) due excessive exploitation wasps. enable_drop = TRUE, fig drop probability calculated logistic function resource_ratio relative threshold. parameter directly represent probability abortion, retained optional alternative fig-retention rule.

title: Parameter guide
text: Scaling constant estimating flower number fig diameter. Represents average number flowers per cm^ alpha.

title: Parameter guide
text: species, named numeric vector giving probabilities oviposition core, mid, outer ovary layers. Sum must 1. “none” given species-specific layer preferences; oviposition attempts allocated according remaining ovary-layer availability. Used use_layering = TRUE.

title: Parameter guide
text: Character vector species names (used extract relevant columns).

title: Parameter guide
text: Defines ecological roles interactions species. Contains three components: guild: character vector assigning species “pollinator”, “galler”, “parasitoid”. hosts: list mapping parasitoids hosts. parasitoid: list mapping hosts attacking parasitoids. structure defines trophic constraints (e.g., parasitoids oviposit unless hosts present).

title: Parameter guide
text: Use egg success probabilities phase. Default TRUE.

title: Parameter guide
text: TRUE, clamps fig diameter fig_diameter_min fig_diameter_max calculating flower number.

title: Parameter guide
text: TRUE, species preferentially oviposit specific layers defined layer_preference.

title: Parameter guide
text: TRUE, fig flowers divided spatial layers (core, mid, outer).

title: Parameter guide
text: Whether activate supplemental parasitism. Default FALSE.

title: Parameter guide
text: TRUE, compute sink-strength metrics alter legacy drop decision. Adds columns: sink_strength, sink_prop, sink_below_min, sink_above_max.

title: Parameter guide
text: Per-ovule sink weight galled ovules (default 1.0).

title: Parameter guide
text: Per-ovule sink weight seeds (default 1.5).

title: Parameter guide
text: Global multiplier sink strength (default 1.0).

title: Parameter guide
text: Reference lower bound sink proportion (default 0.20).

title: Parameter guide
text: Reference upper bound sink proportion (default 0.95).

what: Calculate Alpha, Beta, and Network Metrics from Community Data — calc_all_metrics
title: Calculate Alpha, Beta, and Network Metrics from Community Data — calc_all_metrics
text: function computes key community-level summary metrics fig wasp emergence matrix. includes alpha diversity indices (richness, Shannon, Simpson, evenness), beta diversity (Bray-Curtis, Jaccard), bipartite network structure metrics (nestedness, connectance, linkage density, modularity).

title: Calculate Alpha, Beta, and Network Metrics from Community Data — calc_all_metrics
code: calc_all_metrics(df)

title: Calculate Alpha, Beta, and Network Metrics from Community Data — calc_all_metrics
text: df data.frame row represents fig (sampling unit) column corresponds wasp species. Values species abundances (counts). Column names may include omit prefix "emergence_".

title: Calculate Alpha, Beta, and Network Metrics from Community Data — calc_all_metrics
text: single-row data.frame containing following metrics: mean_richness: Mean number species per fig mean_shannon: Mean Shannon diversity mean_simpson: Mean Simpson diversity mean_evenness: Mean Shannon evenness (Shannon/log(richness)) mean_bray_curtis: Mean Bray-Curtis dissimilarity (beta diversity) mean_jaccard: Mean Jaccard dissimilarity (beta diversity, presence-absence) nestedness: Nestedness based NODF metric (bipartite) connectance: Proportion realized links species-fig network links_per_species: Average number links per species (linkage density) modularity: Modularity score community detection (igraph)

title: Calculate Alpha, Beta, and Network Metrics from Community Data — calc_all_metrics
text: Input typically matrix species emergence abundances across multiple figs, optional "emergence_" prefixes column names. input matrix contains zero-rows, excluded beta diversity network structure calculations. Alpha diversity computed using vegan, beta diversity metrics derived Bray-Curtis Jaccard distances, network metrics calculated using bipartite igraph. Column names "emergence_" prefix automatically stripped. fewer two rows remain removing zero-rows, beta network metrics return NA.

title: Calculate Alpha, Beta, and Network Metrics from Community Data — calc_all_metrics
code: # Simulated emergence data set.seed(1) df <- data.frame(   emergence_A = sample(0:10, 100, replace = TRUE),   emergence_B = sample(0:5, 100, replace = TRUE),   emergence_C = sample(0:3, 100, replace = TRUE) ) metrics <- calc_all_metrics(df) print(metrics) #>   mean_richness mean_shannon mean_simpson mean_evenness mean_bray_curtis #> 1          2.43    0.7033545    0.4440592     0.8378314        0.3962443 #>   mean_jaccard nestedness connectance links_per_species modularity #> 1    0.2963994    27.0643   0.8181818          42.27984 0.07406561

what: figsimR: Simulating Fig Wasp Community Assembly — figsimR-package
title: figsimR: Simulating Fig Wasp Community Assembly — figsimR-package
text: Simulates assembly structure fig wasp communities within fig fruits (Ficus racemosa), capturing ecological processes entry order, parasitism, spatial oviposition constraints, resource limitation, fruit abortion. Designed ecological modeling hypothesis testing using empirical parameters. Current version 0.2.1 integrated "sink strength" theory. Added support species-level `none` entries `layer_preference`, allowing species bypass ovary-layer preference filter. Clarified `layer_preference` refers ovary-layer preference accessibility. Improved handling user-defined fig-wasp communities species interaction systems custom species lists species-specific parameter objects. Retained *Ficus racemosa*-specific defaults worked example allowing users override system-specific parameters.

title: Default Parameter List for Simulating Fig Wasp Communities — parameter_list_default
text: named list following components: entry_mu Named numeric vector mean number individuals species entering fig. entry_size Named numeric vector controlling entry variation; used size NB sdlog lognormal. fecundity_mean Named numeric vector mean number eggs per wasp (.e., per female individual). fecundity_dispersion Named numeric vector fecundity dispersion (size parameter negative binomial). egg_success_prob Named numeric vector baseline oviposition success probabilities (0–1). egg_success_prob_by_phase Nested list success probabilities species--phase combinations. Overrides egg_success_prob enabled. layer_preference Named list giving relative probabilities oviposition fig layers: core, mid, outer. Sum 1. Used use_layering = TRUE. max_entry_table Named numeric vector maximum number individuals species allowed enter fig, adjusted fig size. parasitism_prob Named numeric vector supplemental parasitism probabilities (0–1), used use_supplemental_parasitism = TRUE. entry_priority Named list phases. element character vector species names representing entry order. Entry processed phase. interaction_matrix Square matrix interaction coefficients among species. Used simulate facilitation inhibition. Default zero. interaction_weight Scalar controlling strength interaction effects. Higher values amplify influence interaction_matrix. species_roles list defining ecological roles trophic relationships. Contains: guild Named character vector assigning species guild: "pollinator", "galler", "parasitoid". hosts Named list host species parasitoid. Empty character vector host. parasitoid Named list parasitoids attacking host species. Empty species parasitized.

code: run_fim_reduced_experiment(   version_label,   lhs_param_list,   species_list,   num_figs = 1000,   host_sanction = 0.8,   use_layering = TRUE,   use_layer_preference = TRUE,   use_supplemental_parasitism = FALSE,   enable_drop = TRUE,   use_egg_success_by_phase = TRUE,   drop_cancels_emergence = FALSE,   use_sink_strength = FALSE,   sink_min_prop = 0.2,   sink_max_prop = 0.95,   sink_controls_drop = FALSE,   sink_drop_condition = c("outside_range", "below_min", "above_max"),   seed = 42,   resample_n = 500,   resample_size = 200 )

title: Run a Reduced FIM Simulation and Bootstrap Key Metrics — run_fim_reduced_experiment
text: version_label Character. Label assign output (e.g., "NoLayering"). lhs_param_list List input parameters (entry, fecundity, etc.). species_list List species include network summary. num_figs Integer. Number figs simulate. Default 1000. host_sanction Numeric. Host sanction strength (0–1). use_layering Logical. Whether enable spatial layering. Default TRUE. use_layer_preference Logical. Whether apply layer preference. Default TRUE. use_supplemental_parasitism Logical. Whether activate supplemental parasitism. Default FALSE. enable_drop Logical. Enable legacy drop (host sanction) mechanism. flower use proportion exceeds host_sanction, fig may abort. output "drop_prob" column: Ranges 0 (never drop) 1 (always drop); "is_dropped" column: drop (0) drop (1). sink strength used (use_sink_strength = TRUE) enable_drop = FALSE (host sanction disabled); columns "is_dropped" "drop_prob" outputs meaningful. use_egg_success_by_phase Logical. Use egg success probabilities phase. Default TRUE. drop_cancels_emergence Logical. Whether fruit drop cancels emergence. Default FALSE. use_sink_strength Logical. TRUE, compute sink-strength metrics alter legacy drop decision: use_sink_strength = TRUE enable_drop = FALSE columns "is_dropped" "drop_prob" meaningful. fig dropped value "sink_prop" falls outside thresholds defined sink_min_prop sink_max_prop; columns "sink_below_min" "sink_above_max" logical: TRUE = drop; FALSE = drop. Adds columns: sink_strength, sink_prop, sink_below_min, sink_above_max. Sink strength threshold calculation: sink_strength = sink_linear_coef * (sink_w_gall * galled_total + sink_w_seed * seed_count). sink_min_prop Numeric [0,1]. Reference lower bound sink proportion (default 0.20). sink_max_prop Numeric [0,1]. Reference upper bound sink proportion (default 0.95). sink_controls_drop Logical. TRUE, sink-strength thresholds used mark figs dropped simulation. sink_drop_condition Character. Sink-based drop rule; one "outside_range", "below_min", "above_max". seed Integer. Random seed reproducibility. resample_n Integer. Number bootstrap iterations. Default 500. resample_size Integer. Sample size per bootstrap iteration. Default 200.

text: function simulates fig wasp community structure within set fig fruits, incorporating priority effects, host–parasitoid interactions, resource constraints, pollination, oviposition success, fig abortion mechanisms.

code: simulate_figwasp_community(   num_figs = 1000,   fig_diameter_mean = 2.5,   fig_diameter_sd = 1.2,   k = 300,   alpha = 1.3,   max_entry_table = NULL,   fecundity_mean,   fecundity_dispersion,   entry_mu,   entry_size,   entry_priority,   species_roles,   entry_distribution = "lognormal",   interaction_matrix = NULL,   interaction_weight = 0,   seed = NULL,   egg_success_prob = NULL,   egg_success_prob_by_phase = NULL,   parasitism_prob = NULL,   layer_preference = NULL,   p_pollination_per_ovule = 0.98,   p_no_entry = 0.002,   host_sanction = 0.8,   fig_diameter_min = 1.3,   fig_diameter_max = 4,   record_individual = FALSE,   use_layering = TRUE,   use_layer_preference = TRUE,   enable_drop = TRUE,   drop_cancels_emergence = FALSE,   use_supplemental_parasitism = FALSE,   use_flower_limit = TRUE,   use_egg_success_by_phase = TRUE,   use_sink_strength = FALSE,   sink_w_gall = 1,   sink_w_seed = 1.5,   sink_linear_coef = 1,   sink_min_prop = 0.2,   sink_max_prop = 0.95 )

title: Simulate Fig Wasp Community Assembly (Observed on Ficus racemosa) — simulate_figwasp_community
text: num_figs Integer. Number fig fruits simulate (.e., number independent communities, default = 1000). fig treated discrete community. fig_diameter_mean Numeric. Mean fig diameter (cm, default = 2.5). used determine ovule number. Affects resource availability. fig_diameter_sd Numeric. Standard deviation fig diameter normal distribution used generate fig diameters, default = 1.2). k Numeric. Scaling constant estimating flower number fig diameter (default = 300). Controls ovule-based competition. alpha Numeric. Exponent diameter–flower power-law used estimate flower number fig diameter; controls shape seed-yield curve (default = 1.3). max_entry_table Named numeric vector giving baseline maximum number arrivals oviposition-attempting wasps per species. Names must match species_roles$guild. NULL, function uses default Ficus racemosa values supplied species set matches worked example; otherwise, generic value assigned species, warning recommending system-specific values. fecundity_mean mean potential number eggs per individual wasp. fecundity_dispersion Named numeric vectors fecundity distribution. dispersion parameter negative binomial distribution modeling individual fecundity. entry_mu Named numeric vectors controlling expected number wasp arrival oviposition-attempt events per fig. pollinators, corresponds physical entry fig cavity; non-pollinating fig wasps, represents arrival fig oviposition attempts outside fig wall. entry_mu interpreted physical entry fig cavity species. entry_size Named numeric vectors controlling entry distribution (dispersion arrival oviposition-attempt distribution). See entry_distribution. entry_distribution = "nb": used NB size parameter. lognormal: used derive sdlog = 1 / sqrt(entry_size). negative binomial option, corresponds "size" parameter; smaller values produce stronger aggregation greater among-fig variation. lognormal option, controls log-scale variance arrival process. parameter sample size represent number eggs. entry_priority Named list phases species. species_roles List $guild, $hosts, $parasitoid. entry_distribution "nb" "lognormal". See entry_size. interaction_matrix Optional numeric matrix (pairwise interactions). interaction_weight Numeric. Strength interaction effects. seed RNG seed (optional). egg_success_prob Global probability single oviposition attempt results successful egg. Applies use_egg_success_by_phase = FALSE. egg_success_prob_by_phase Named list species-specific, phase-specific egg-success probabilities. element named numeric vector whose names correspond phases entry_priority. use_egg_success_by_phase = TRUE, matching species-phase value overrides baseline value egg_success_prob. phase-specific value provided, baseline egg_success_prob used; also missing, default success probability 1. parasitism_prob Named numeric vector supplemental parasitism. layer_preference Named list specifying species-specific ovary-layer preference accessibility. species can assigned either numeric vector giving relative probabilities core, mid, outer ovary layers; none indicate species-specific ovary-layer preference. NULL, figsimR uses default Ficus racemosa-specific layer preference supplied species set matches worked example; otherwise, species assigned none users encouraged provide system-specific values. p_pollination_per_ovule Numeric [0–1]. Probability unoccupied ovule becomes seed. p_no_entry Numeric [0–1]. Probability fig receives entries. host_sanction Numeric [0–1]. Legacy overuse threshold drop logic. fig_diameter_min Numeric. Minimum fig diameter used cap values (optional). Truncation bounds (used). Truncate simulated diameters (thus flower counts) biologically reasonable range. fig_diameter_max Numeric. Maximum fig diameter used cap values (optional). Truncation bounds (used). Truncate simulated diameters (thus flower counts) biologically reasonable range. record_individual Logical. TRUE, per-individual oviposition recorded. use_layering Logical switches layering. TRUE, fig flowers divided spatial layers (core, mid, outer). use_layer_preference Logical. TRUE, apply species-specific ovary-layer preference accessibility values supplied layer_preference. FALSE, ovary-layer resources still used use_layering = TRUE, species-specific layer preference applied. enable_drop Logical. Enable legacy drop (host sanction) mechanism. flower use proportion exceeds host_sanction, fig may abort. output "drop_prob" column: Ranges 0 (never drop) 1 (always drop); "is_dropped" column: drop (0) drop (1). sink strength used (use_sink_strength = TRUE) enable_drop = FALSE (host sanction disabled); columns "is_dropped" "drop_prob" outputs meaningful. drop_cancels_emergence Logical. FALSE (figs dropped wasps emerge), dropped figs yield zero output (wasps emerge end phase). use_supplemental_parasitism Logical. Whether activate supplemental parasitism. Default FALSE. use_flower_limit Logical. TRUE, clamps fig diameter fig_diameter_min fig_diameter_max calculating flower number. use_egg_success_by_phase Logical. Use egg success probabilities phase. Default TRUE. use_sink_strength Logical. TRUE, compute sink-strength metrics alter legacy drop decision: use_sink_strength = TRUE enable_drop = FALSE columns "is_dropped" "drop_prob" meaningful. fig dropped value "sink_prop" falls outside thresholds defined sink_min_prop sink_max_prop; columns "sink_below_min" "sink_above_max" logical: TRUE = drop; FALSE = drop. Adds columns: sink_strength, sink_prop, sink_below_min, sink_above_max. Sink strength threshold calculation: sink_strength = sink_linear_coef * (sink_w_gall * galled_total + sink_w_seed * seed_count). sink_w_gall Numeric [0, +). Per-ovule sink weight galled ovules (default 1.0). sink_w_seed Numeric [0, +). Per-ovule sink weight seeds (default 1.5). sink_linear_coef Numeric. Global multiplier sink strength (default 1.0). sink_min_prop Numeric [0,1]. Reference lower bound sink proportion (default 0.20). sink_max_prop Numeric [0,1]. Reference upper bound sink proportion (default 0.95).
